title: papualthia urdanetensis
intro: Papualthia urdanetensis, also known as Urandan Pawpaw or Urandan, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Philippines and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The medicinal properties of Papualthia urdanetensis include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat wounds. Herbal preparations of Papualthia urdanetensis include a decoction made from its roots and leaves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
